Issue No.2] • April 2013
How To Clear 6-Figures Per Year
Writing A Couple Ernails Per Day
What up Email Player...
Last Fall I met a woman who was quite ... fascinated ... by my lifestyle. She works a
full time corporate job and she was intrigued by how my day basically consisted (at the
time --lately I've been on a roll with projects) of getting up at whatever time I want,
writing a couple emails (and sometimes none at all), then going to the beach, taking a
shower and doing something fun (golf, road trip, hanging out with whoever, etc) the rest of
the day. Very little stress, very little drama and very little angsting. I take frequent road
trips, days off, and have complete control over my schedule and life. No, I'm not the
richest person in class (not even close), but I earn more than enough to satisfy my desires
(this newsletter being just one of many income streams using email-- I don't just teach
this stuff, I DO it), and am steadily growing and will continue to do so, God willing.
Anyway, she started calling it "The Ben Settle Lifestyle" (ha) and would ask me how she
could do the same thing. Ironically (funny how these things work) around that time I also
got asked a question from one of my website subscribers:
"I would like to know... It [Email Players] seems like it's email
copywriting training only. But do you also share the business
model you use... so that we can actually get the traffic and live
the lifestyle."

Part 1:
Foundational Stuff
1. Wealth Is An lncoJDe Stream.
This is something one of Gary Bencivenga's clients told him. He was selling
newsletters for the client. And the client said they weren't in the publishing business, they
were in the renewal business. When customers renewed it was making a sale again
without doing anything. This is why in my opinion (and I'm right) you should sell
continuity of some kind. The key to selling continuity is EMAIL (daily!) and selling
something of such high value peoples' lives are changed by it. It can be a newsletter,
membership site, licensing your products to people, supplements (careful with this though,
to me it's not worth the red tape), a CD-of-the-month, video-of-the-month, etc. The idea
is, you want to build pipelines and not carry buckets. This is an analogy I heard
from Robert Kiyosaki. To free up your time, you put a lot of time and energy into
building a pipeline that brings water to you, instead of you laboriously hauling buckets of
water back and forth.
This is essential to understand if you want to live the email lifestyle.
Ideally, what you want are multiple continuity income streams with different ways
the money is distributed to you (i.e. merchant account, PayPal, ClickBank, check fromJV
partner, etc -- at least one that's not connected to your social security number). That way
if one goes down, another picks right up without you missing a beat. I won't say this is
easy. Building a pipeline is often time and energy draining. But, you only do it ONCE. As
opposed to getting up and hauling buckets back and forth from the lake day after day. So
you do the work one time and there may be some periodic "maintenance", but it's mosdy
automatic. And, it should be a product you're passionate about that solves problems
you're passionate about solving.
Hey, don't scoff. I'm spending a lot of time on this because it's mucho important.
Get this right and you can spend your days writing an email or two and having more fun

than a stoned hippie at Denny's. You won't have to obsess as much about how many sales
you're getting on a particular month. Do it right and you will be in a position where
you're like, "crap, what do I do with all this extra time???" (A good problem to have, eh?)
2. Money Is Attracted To Speed (Not PerfectionisDl)
This is mostly for ye olde copywriters reading this. When you work for yourself,
you do NOT need to obsess over every word of your sales pitches and emails like Gollum
stroking the One Ring in his cave of gloom. In fact, that will hold you back and guarantee
you make far less $$ than you would otherwise. So forget perfectionism. I'm not saying to
be sloppy. And you should always bend this to your market. But you want speed, Speed,
SPEED! I remember even Gary Halbert saying while he and john Carlton were world
class copywriters, they were not nearly as good (or wealthy) entrepreneurs as Dan
Kennedy who was far faster and way less concerned with perfectionism. Good advice.
Don't worry. While you WILL need sales letters for your products, I will give you ideas on
how to do them FAST later in this issue when we get to the tactical stuff. In the
meantime...
3. Scalability
Finally, always make your operations scalable. By that I mean, you can grow your
business and handle lots of sales in a way where it takes little or zero of your time. Don't
mail anything yourself. Outsource everything you are not absolutely essential at
(programming, fulfillment, shipping, etc). That way you can just keep adding names to
your buyers list and you make more and more sales without having to worry about the
manual labor aspect of dealing with all the administration. Very important. You want to
shift from self employed to business owner. So when you are ready, you can buy lots of
traffic, handle lots of sales, and be able to take off for months at a time and come back to
find your business going just as strong (or even stronger!) than when you left.
Like I said, these foundational concepts are not super advanced brain busters. But
hardly anyone thinks about them or understands how powerful these 3 concepts are.
Frankly, JUST applying these 3 concepts to your business each day (and to everything you
do in bid'niz) is like semi-advanced marketing training, and will put you on a path that
takes you way farther than you would otherwise, with way less effort.

Part 2:
Market Research Stuff
The next step is to pick your market carefully and strategize your products to sell.
Again, in my opinion (and I'm right) you should sell some kind of continuity. That doesn't
mean you have to sell continuity up front (although you can certainly try, especially if you
are in a HOT market and have your email skillz down). But continuity is important. And
if nothing else you can simply sell an ebook or other product on the front end, and back
end your continuity.
Very clean, and very simple.
But before you do this, you will have to do some heavy market research.
There is no way around this. Yes, it can be tedious. And it helps if you're "in" the
market you sell to in this case.
But even if you are, the exercise of researching your market will help you in ways
you cannot possibly fathom right now (but will later). Knowing your market better than
anyone else means you will never run out of ideas for emails. You will be able to create
the perfect products just for them. And, if it's a market you're passionate about (very
important) you will get a lot of enjoyment and fun out of the process. You won't dread
writing them emails you will look forward to it. You will relish the fan mail you get from
people whose lives you've changed. And you will find a certain kind of contentment you
just can't experience any other way. Now, that said, you may be wondering how you get to
"know" your market. And so what I'm going to do is list a series of questions for you to
answer (in as much detail as possible) about your market. When you fill this out (don't be
lazy! this is mucho important...) it should be obvious what product to sell and your head
should be swimming with ideas for emails to write to them (as every problem, desire and
fantasy they have is good email "fodder"). OK, without further ado here are the questions
to answer. Do whatever you have to do (including talking to people IN the market with
the problems you want to solve if you can swing it) to answer them all in as much detail as
possible. Doing so will be worth solid gold to you and make the rest of the process a lot
easier:

• 	What keeps them AWAKE at night?

• 	What's their most URGENT, "hot button" crisis right now?
• 	Who are their ENEMmS?
• 	What INCOME bracket are they in?

• 	What do they WANT more than anything?

• 	Who wears the PANTS in the family when it comes to making buying decisions?

• 	What kind of SLANG do they use in every day conversation?
• 	Who ELSE is successfully selling something similar to them, and how?
• 	What are the POSITIVE emotions the market has?
• 	What BOOKS are they reading? (Do they even read books??)
• 	What MOvms are they watching?

"Don't tellJDe about your weed killer,
tell1ne about ~ny crab grass!"
In other words ... you want to be able to fluently and intelligently talk about their

negotiator Jim Camp says) you are safe. You'll never bore them, lose their interest, or be
ignored. And, I will add, creating products and writing emails to sell them the solutions to
those problems becomes much easier.

Part 3:
Tactical Stuff
This is where the rubber meets the dough ($$). Where you can now scale your
business to as big as you want without suffering from eternal burnout or running around
crazy from the butt crack of dawn until late into the night. Instead, you have everything
prepped and ready -- with orders taken automatically (whether digitally or using a printer
like I use at www.selbymarketing.coDl if you sell physical products).
So, how complicated is this part?
Not complicated at all.
In fact, it's actually kinda easy and simple.
Check this out:
Back in 2008 Ken McCarthy hired me to write the bullets for the CD's of his
System Seminar that year. And one of the teachings I was to write bullets for was an "Info
marketing for beginners, training Ken did with a million dollar marketer named Uoyd
Irvin. They talked about lots of cool stuff, but their focus on simplicity stood out most.
And it was this one lesson that I have kept in my head ever since, never veering from it,
and never letting myself get caught up in anything complicated. Remember, money is
attracted to SPEED. Anyway, people were asking about social media and all the sexy bells
& whistles they see going on out there in Internet marketing goo-roo land. What was Ken
and Uoyd's response? They still do the basics:
• 	Squeeze page
• 	Sales letter
• 	Email follow up
Hardly exciting, is it?
Yet, between the two of them, they do pretty well. Well over 7 figures per year.
And this, my fine furry friend, is how you create that email lifestyle. You take all the

"prep" work (you only have to do once time) and then simply do the three things above:
Create a squeeze page and sales letter, and then spend your work day writing an email to

Now, for specifics:
You do NOT have to go crazy at first. Just get everything up. A squeeze page is
easy to do. You can also belt out a sales pitch relatively easy. Remember, when you do
email the Settle way a sales letter is important but not necessary to be perfect. If you're
really in a hurry and don't want to hire a copywriter just do it Michael Senoff style. Here
is the template:
• 	Headline (which is just the title of the product, nothing fancy or "killer")
• 	"Here's what you get... " (yes, word for word, this is what he puts under the
headline) with either teaser bullets of what's in the product or you can just describe
the components of your product -- i.e. 350 page book, bonus report, guarantee,
etc). To see examples see any of the products at:
www.hardtofindseminars.com/ seminars.htm
• 	Order link (self explanatory)
Yes it can be THAT simple.
You can do that in probably less than 10 minutes.
Will this sell better than a real sales letter?
Probably not, but it'll get the job done and it's fast and that's what's important (you
can always go back and do another one, but just get it out there for now). Once that's up
you can now just focus on the last (and most important) part: Email follow up. This is
where your investment in this newsletter comes in so handy. I am constantly handing you
ideas and templates. Go back through your issues and take advantage of it. That's what
it's there for.
The only other part now is traffic and list building. We've talked about list building

Part 4:
lm.plem.entation Stuff
Now, let's bring this all together in a way where you can get started and see results
quickly. What I suggest (no, I INSIST) you do is use Sean D'Souza's "sequential selling"
model.
Don't argue with me about this.
Just do it.
Especially if you are starting from scratch (if you already have your own strategy
you want to use, fine, but I would bet you green money Sean's way would work much
better.) Sean doesn't claim to have invented it (it's been around for thousands of years,
and is now even used by companies like Apple and Amazon, as well as every restaurant,
karate school, religion, etc). But he's one of the only (maybe the only) people teaching it.
I wrote about this way of selling in last August's "Email Players" issue.

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
If you have not read it, here is what I said:
I 	learned this from Sean D'Souza (one of the great -- and probably most
underrated -- marketing minds of our time) while listening to the 2008
System Seminar CO's. 	It is extremely powerful, 	but can be a bit tricky
to figure out at first. 	It's based on the way we're all used to
consuming information 	(or food or anything else) 	in real life -- where
you follow a specific "sequence" you're using whether you realize it or
not.
One example would be movies.
You don't see "The Empire Strikes Back" before you see "Star Wars",
right? Because you don't really know what's going on or get the full
context of the movie. 	So instead you watch "Star Wars" and if you like
it, 	you are going to automatically want to see 	(i.e. 	pay for) 	"The
Empire Strikes Back" and then, 	if you like that, 	"Return Of The Jedi".
So by seeing the FIRST movie off the bat, 	and not jumping the sequence,
you are almost a shoe-in to buy tickets to the next two movies. 	Those
sales are ALREADY made before they even see a sales pitch! 	(Again,
assuming they had a good experience with the first movie). Another is
Karate schools. 	You don't 	just walk in and say, 	"can I have a green
belt please?" No, 	no no ••• 	they'll probably round house kick you right
out the door. 	Instead, 	you 	(and everyone else -- no exceptions) 	start
with the WHITE belt. And then you progress to the next color, 	and the
next color -- and, 	like with "Star Wars" -- you know exactly where you
are in the sequence at all times. 	You know what to buy first. 	You know
what to buy next. And you may even have goals to buy more down the line
(those "sales" are already made). Another example: 	Restaurants! 	Go to
any restaurant. 	The first "sale" is seating you at the table. 	You can't
order your food and eat it while waiting to be seated. Then, when
you're seated you get an appetizer. Then the meal. Then the dessert.
Then coffee, 	etc. 	It's a VERY specific sequence designed to extract as
much money out of your hungry little hand as possible 	(they start with
free, 	then inexpensive, 	then pricier main course, 	then back down to
less expensive ••• 	it's very clever ••• ) But that's okay, 	because that's
why you're there. 	You don't resent it, 	you enjoy it. 	Just like you
eagerly can't wait to see "Empire Strikes Back" after "Star
Wars" 	(assuming you liked "Star Wars"), 	etc. Amazon.com does this, 	too.
You MUST register to buy something on Amazon. 	You cannot skip the
sequence. 	First you register for free, 	then you can buy, 	etc. Apple
Computer does it to a degree as well -- free iTunes software, 	then 99
cent music, 	then you might buy an !Phone, 	iPad or laptop ••• 	then they
de-escalate 	(Sean D'Souza has this all figured out) 	to less expensive
accessories ••• making the experience very easy and non-threatening.

OK, 	so let's break this down for people like us.
I think it is extremely powerful to have ONE front end product, 	and
then not try to sell people anything else until they've bought that
front end product which should be your flagship product. 	The product
that brings them in the right way, 	sells them on you and your "ways",
that puts them in your world and gives them a great experience, 	and
then naturally 	(and logically) 	leads them to sale #2.
Sean does it like this:
First, 	he makes you opt in. 	You cannot buy any of his products until
you've opted in. 	That is the first "conversion" 	(he has a great theory
about what he calls "the 3rd conversion", where once someone gets to
the 3rd conversion, 	they will buy everything you sell, 	and I 	think the
gawd awful Star Wars prequels are a perfect example of this -- you have
to REALLY like the original series to sit through the prequels ••• )
After you opt in, 	you can ONLY buy his 	"Brain Audit" product. 	You
cannot buy his other products -- not even his $10k productl 	-- until
you've bought the first one. 	And, 	I 	believe he even makes you subscribe
to his continuity next before he'll let you buy anything else. 	It's
pure brilliance and genius and he's the only person I 	know of who
teaches this.
For BenSettle.com I 	follow this for the most part. Although I 	kind of
"cheat" sometimes by putting links to "Email Players" in my blog posts
(very few people subscribe to "Email Players" until they've opted in
and have been on my list for a little bit, 	anyway) 	and selling an
affiliate product once in a while. 	I 	also have a sales letter for
"Copywriting Grab Bag" out there, 	and sometimes people find it on their
own, 	but that's fine. 	Because "Email Players" get it at a discount and
are my main focus, 	and if someone buys CGB, 	fine. 	But the point is 	(and
the reason it works so well is) we're already conditioned to consume
things this way -- whether it be food, 	information or anything else.
It's very easy on the brain as your customer always knows where he is,
what to do next, 	and what direction to go in your product line. 	You're
also doing them a big favor as they are more likely to consume your
product this way 	(all of your products) 	and not let them sit on a shelf
or whatever, 	which 	(if your product is tops) will improve their lives,
sell them on you 	(and only buying from you) 	and get them wanting all
your other products.
OK, 	so that's the sequential selling method. 	I 	HIGHLY suggest reading
sean's site 	(he may even have a product teaching this by now) 	at
www.PsychoTactics.com.

-- 13 of 28 --

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
I KNOW this seems like a lot of moving parts if you are not familiar with it. And
like I said, you should read this issue several times to let everything sink in. But, once you
got it, this is VERY simple. Now, let's talk about some of the ways you can apply all of
this. Specifically, 5 kinds of funnels you can build to make this go smoothly. These aren't
the ONLY ways, but they will get the job done, and will likely fit into many different kinds
of peoples' preferences as far as how they want to run a business:
1. Opt in-product-continuity-ancillary back end products
This is pretty straight forward. You send traffic to an opt in page, then email them
daily to sell a flagship type product. Then, you back end those buyers your continuity.
Once in continuity you can sell them other products. Again, you don't have to do all this
work yoursel£ It's perfectly okay to hire or JV with an email writer to do this.
2. Opt in-continuity-ancillary back end products
This is what I do with BenSettle.com. I front end the "Email Players" newsletter
(this does NOT work for a lot of markets and you must have your skillz razor sharp to
pull it ofl) and back end sell my other products in the newsletter (via the enclosed catalog
you get each month).
3. Opt in-continuity (no back end products)
I'm playing around with this now. And the idea is, I'm going to drive traffic to an
opt in page and then finding a continuity product I can sell as an affiliate. If the stick rate
is high enough this is a great way to get some side income streams coming in where you
do work once and never touch it again. So imagine, for example, having 3-4 lists with 90
or so emails working hard to sell continuity you don't have to fulfill. You just cash checks.
Pretty cool idea, eh? I'll let you know how this goes once I get it going...
4. Opt in via direct sales-continuity-ancillary back end products (or just
ancillary products and no continuity, however you prefer)
One of my former clients does this since the numbers work out so well. He buys
something like 1Ok or more visitors a day to his flagship product sales letter and does
NOT ask them to opt in. Instead, he just wants BUYERS. So he doesn't care if they click
away or not. Those buyers are then sent emails selling his other products. He used to sell
-14-
www.BcnScttle.com 	• 	Copyright 2013 Ben Settle, All Rights ReseJVed 	• 	Published By Settle, LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 28 --

THE 	EMAIL PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
continuity, but now I believe he doesn't. You can do it either way if you want, although I
recommend selling continuity when it makes sense.
5. Opt in-ancillary back end products (no continuity)
Ironically, I used do this in the prostate market before I licensed that business over
to someone else recently. (Sounds strange probably, since I've been railing about
continuity, but in this market I just didn't want to mess with it). Anyway, he.JV's with a
traffic guy and they send ALL traffic to an opt in page. They opt in and are sent an email
every day for 94 days. Those buyers then get a daily email selling them ancillary affiliate
products, etc (currently they are not sold continuity but I'm not sure what their plans are
for it now). I wrote all the auto-responder emails a couple years ago and so they don't
touch it (it pulls well, so why mess with it?) And they JV with another email writer to sell
the ancillary products. Anyway, as you can see, there's lots of ways to skin this cat. What I
suggest is picking the ONE above that's most appealing to you, your work ethic, and your
preferences and just run with it. There is no one size fits all and I do more than one way.
However, I started with ONE way. Do not get bogged down with details here. That's why
I say read this issue multiple times so you ingrain the concepts, then apply. And to help
with this, I want to now break this down step-by-step for you -- kinda like a cheat sheet of
everything in this issue:
1. 	Pick the right market-- one you're passionate about helping the people in and
study it by filling out all the questions in this issue
2. 	Create your product and continuity -- see enclosed "Crypto Marketing
Newsletter" issue for help and guidance
3. 	Create your opt in page and sales page -either hire someone, write it
yourself or use the Senoff model if need be (you want to get this going FAST--
and it does NOT have to be perfect or even that good, quite frankly, as your emails
should do most of the heavy lifting).
4. 	Start bullding a list and mailing it-- sending them to your sales letter each
day until they buy, then back end sell your continuity and other products
(depending on the funnel you want to use out of the 5 options above)
5. 	Outsource or JV with people/businesses when possible -- especially
-15-
www.BenSettle.com 	• 	Copyright 2013 Ben Settle, All Rights Resctvcd 	• 	Published By Settle, LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 28 --

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
doing tedious things or things you hate doing (or suck at doing). So, for example,
team up with a traffic guy. Outsource product mailing and fulfillment.
And that's it.
Of course, the devil is in the details, yeah? So make sure you read this issue several
times. That is, if you want to live a lifestyle you can build relatively quickly and simply,
where you control your time and your "work day" is mostly reduced to writing an email
or two.
Hey, I know this is a lot to take in so just keep it simple and if you have questions
ASK. Take advantage of that built-in perk to your "Email Players" subscription.
I'm here to help and want to see as many of my subscribers do this as possible.
Word up,
Ben Settle

The Bible's Secret Email Lesson
One of history's coolest marketers is Bruce Barton . 	Back in his day (early to
mid 1900's) he was a household name, a giant in the advertising busine ss and
even an advisor to presidents.
And he was an AWESOME teacher .
One of his best teachings (in my humble , but accurate, opinion) was in a 1924
radio broadcast about when the Biblical patriarch Joseph was the second in
command in Egy pt.
Joseph was •it . •
Egypt's top dawg.
Everyone was commanded by Pharaoh to follow his orders and his name was as
familiar to every Egyptian man, woman and child as their own, until...
•And Joseph died... and there
a ro se up a new king over Egypt
which knew not Joseph . •
(Exodus 1 : 6 -8)

In a matter of a couple decades, all Joseph's power, prestige and name
recognition vanished like a fa r t 	in the wind. He went from being "the man" to
being a footnote in some hieroglyphic somewhere - 	completely forgotten .
There's a HUGE lesson here for entrepreneurs.
And that is this whole idea of how easy it is to be forgotten. Happens ALL the
time. 	One day you're "Joseph" and everyone in your market knows who you are ...
the next they've forgotten you or have found a new king to hang with (and buy
from) .
Anyway, t h is is why I' m so big on email .
When you do it right, 	it's almost impossible for your list to forget about you
(in fact, you'll many times make "top of mind" status).
Yes ... I've beat this drum to death this week .
But this is the #1 skill to have, IMHO .
Nothing else even comes close .
Which is why the first crypto Marketing Newsletter issue is ALL about email,
and contains some extremely simple (and fun) ways to write emails people love
to read and buy from.
